sections.title: A: Word formation
sections.instructions: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

items.sentence: My grandad had a long career. He started work as an ...... (ASSIST) and worked his way to the top.
He was very ...... (SUCCESS).
He ...... (SAVE) the company from disaster many times.
When he was in charge he was never ...... (BOSS).
He understood people and all the ...... (EMPLOY) admired him.
He was an engineer, which is quite a difficult ...... (OCCUPY), and for 40 years he worked on many projects.
The company had an ...... (OFFICE) retirement age of 65.
At 65, he reached the age of ...... (RETIRE).
He was very well known to other engineers – you might even say he was ...... (FAME)!
He was always very busy when he was working, but now he's ...... (RETIRE), he's got a lot more time to spend with his grandchildren!
Mr Robinson is responsible ......
options: A on how long you've been working here.
B about the new time of the meeting.
C to last year as 'a disaster'.
E for a job to make a good impression.
F for interviewing people applying for jobs.
G as a racing driver, or maybe a car mechanic.
H with her job and is thinking of finding a new one.

sections.title: C: Key Word Transformation
sections.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.

items.sentence:
It's so hot today that I can't work! **too**
It's ...... me to work today!

I can't go to bed late because I've got to work in the morning. **up**
I can't ...... late because I've got to work in the morning.

You're too young to join the army. **old**
You're ...... to join the army.

Why did the boss cancel the meeting? **off**
Why did the boss ...... the meeting?
This job is ...... difficult that I need help.
It was ...... a hot day that we went to the beach.
She isn't old ...... to apply for the job.
The office is ...... noisy for me to concentrate.
He runs ...... than his brother.
This is ...... interesting book I've ever read.
The new office is much ...... than the old one.
She earns ...... than I do.
He was ...... tired to finish the report.
It was ...... great weather that we stayed an extra day.
Your work is getting ...... and .......
He isn't experienced ...... to be a manager.
It was ...... a stressful meeting that nobody spoke afterwards.
The earlier train is ...... than the later one.

sections.title: E: Complete the sentences
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: She applied ...... a job as a marketing manager.
He was offered a ...... – his salary went up by 10%.
I've decided to ...... my job and start my own business.
She had an ...... for the position of head teacher.
He works very long ...... – sometimes twelve hours a day.
She earns a good ...... as a doctor.
He got the sack because he was always ...... for work.
She is self-employed, so she works for ......
The company has decided to ...... on new staff.
He has to ...... a lot of pressure in his job.
She ...... her CV and sent it to several companies.
He was made ...... when the factory closed down.
She decided to ...... a career change after ten years in banking.
He is very ...... – he organises everything perfectly at work.
